questions.question_text: Generate the number pattern from $3x$ for $x = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5$.

options.why: Read $3x$ as $x + 3$, adding the $3$ to each input instead of multiplying by it.
Started substituting at $x = 0$ instead of $x = 1$, shifting every term one place.
Listed the input values $x$ instead of the outputs $3x$.

questions.question_text: Generate the number pattern from $2x + 3$ for $x = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5$.

options.why: Started substituting at $x = 0$, so the first term is $2(0) + 3 = 3$.
Added before multiplying, evaluating $2(x + 3)$ instead of $2x + 3$.

questions.question_text: Generate the number pattern from $3x - 7$ for $x = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5$.

options.why: Subtracted in the wrong order, evaluating $7 - 3x$ instead of $3x - 7$.
Started substituting at $x = 0$, so the first term is $3(0) - 7 = -7$.
Dropped the negative signs, writing the first two terms as positive.

questions.question_text: Generate the number pattern from $10 - 2x$ for $x = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5$.

options.why: Started substituting at $x = 0$, so the first term is $10 - 2(0) = 10$.
Reversed the subtraction, evaluating $2x - 10$ instead of $10 - 2x$.

questions.question_text: The number pattern comes from $2x + 3$. Find its value when $x = 20$.

options.why: Substituted into $2x$ only and forgot to add the constant $3$.
Added the coefficient instead of multiplying, evaluating $20 + 2 + 3$.

questions.question_text: The number pattern comes from $5 - 4x$. Find its value when $x = 12$.

options.why: Subtracted in the wrong order, evaluating $4x - 5 = 48 - 5$.
Substituted into $-4x$ only and dropped the $+5$.
